auth_notes: Authentication typically requires a Bearer token provided via the Canary Partner Portal or by an account manager. Some endpoints may require a Property-ID header in addition to the authorization token.
rate_limits: Typically 10-20 requests per second depending on the specific partner agreement and endpoint.
gotchas: Documentation is often restricted to verified partners and may not be fully accessible to public developers without an account.
The API is heavily dependent on the underlying Property Management System (PMS) integration, which can cause latency in data updates.
Webhook setup usually requires manual configuration within the Canary dashboard or coordination with their support team.
client_must_provide: Client ID (for OAuth flows if applicable)
Access to the Canary Dashboard for webhook registration
research_notes: Implementation will require the n8n HTTP Request node. Users should set up a 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n to handle the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' requirement. For webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node and provide the resulting URL to Canary's technical team or input it into their developer settings.

effort_recommendation.rationale: Implementation will require the n8n HTTP Request node. Users should set up a 'Header Auth' credential type in n8n to handle the 'Authorization: Bearer <token>' requirement. For webhooks, use the n8n Webhook node and provide the resulting URL to Canary's technical team or input it into their developer settings.
